name: Modi
description: Modi, whose name translates to 'Courage' or 'Wrathful,' is a Norse god associated with courage, battle, and berserker rage. He is the son of Thor, the god of thunder, and the giantess Járnsaxa. Modi is destined to survive Ragnarok, the end of the world in Norse mythology, along with his brother Magni. After Ragnarok, Modi and Magni are prophesied to inherit their father's hammer, Mjolnir, signifying their strength and responsibility in rebuilding the world. While not as prominently featured as some other Norse deities, Modi embodies the qualities of bravery and fierce determination. His role in the post-Ragnarok world highlights the continuation of strength and valor within the new order. Accounts of Modi are relatively scarce, but his lineage and prophesied role underscore his importance as a symbol of resilience and the enduring power of the Aesir gods. His potential is seen in his future wielding of Mjolnir, implying a great capacity for wielding divine power. Modi exemplifies a continuation of the warrior spirit and a future of courage in the face of adversity.
subtitle: God of Courage and Post-Ragnarok Strength

keyMyths.title: The Lineage of Modi
keyMyths.description: Modi is the son of Thor and the giantess Járnsaxa, inheriting strength from both sides of his family. This lineage marks him as a powerful warrior.

keyMyths.title: Survival of Ragnarok
keyMyths.description: Modi is prophesied to survive Ragnarok along with his brother Magni, highlighting his importance for the world's future. They will inherit Mjolnir.

keyMyths.title: Inheriting Mjolnir
keyMyths.description: After Ragnarok, Modi and Magni inherit Mjolnir from their father Thor, symbolizing their strength and responsibility. This signifies a new era.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, outlining the stories and roles of various gods.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, outlining the stories and roles of various gods.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems that provide insights into Norse mythology and cosmology.
